verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: G1-G10 each has a named test; Tests reference invariant IDs; Gauntlet handed to [[tasks/yzx-iso/t10-0-lane-index]]; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates committed red at lifeos a82c166 (3/3 failing: spec absent), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.naming_evidence: Spec section 5 defines CT-G1..CT-G10 — one named, concrete, runnable conformance test per goal (host-upgrade-reboot-zero-loss, native-latency-benchmark, kill-reboot-byte-identical, no-durable-on-run, zero-home-owners-sweep, clean-host-boot, cold-reboot-reattach, acquire-release-cycle, nix-omada-no-docker, governed-update-hold), each with its definition and runner lane.
gate_result.reference_evidence: Every CT-G row cites the invariant ids it proves (e.g. CT-G1 -> I01/I05, CT-G8 -> I11/I12/I13), and every cited id exists in the I01..I17 ledger.
gate_result.handoff_evidence: The complete CT-G1..CT-G10 gauntlet against a release candidate is handed to tasks/yzx-iso/t10-0-lane-index (ARCHBP-118..120/134/135).
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: tests/archbp-062-conformance-tests.spec.ts validates the presence of all ten named tests, their invariant references against the real ledger, and the t10 handoff in the published spec.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-062 is complete: each goal G1-G10 has a named, concrete, runnable conformance test CT-G1..CT-G10 defined in the ratified spec — e.g. CT-G1 proves G1 by running a host apt full-upgrade plus reboot and verifying the LifeOS durable tier byte-identical — every test references the invariant ids it proves, and the end-to-end gauntlet is handed to the t10 release lane.
